snippets.title: Cross-entropy from scratch (discrete)
snippets.code:
import numpy as np

p = np.array([0.5, 0.5])          # true PMF P
q = np.array([0.9, 0.1])          # model PMF Q

# H(P, Q) = -sum p(x) log q(x)
ce = -np.sum(p * np.log(q))
print("H(P,Q) nats:", ce)
print("H(P) nats:", -np.sum(p * np.log(p)))
snippets.explanation: Uses natural log (nats). When p and q differ, H(P, Q) exceeds H(P) by D_KL(P || Q).

snippets.title: Categorical cross-entropy loss for one example
snippets.code:
import numpy as np

logits = np.array([2.0, 1.0, 0.5])
probs = np.exp(logits - logits.max())
probs /= probs.sum()  # softmax Q

y_true = 1  # class index
loss = -np.log(probs[y])
print("pi:", probs)
print("loss -log pi_y:", loss)
snippets.explanation: For one-hot P, cross-entropy reduces to −log q(y). Softmax turns logits into Q.

snippets.title: PyTorch CrossEntropyLoss (multi-class)
snippets.code:
import torch
import torch.nn as nn

logits = torch.tensor([[2.0, 1.0, 0.5]])
target = torch.tensor([1])  # class index

loss_fn = nn.CrossEntropyLoss()
print("loss:", loss_fn(logits, target).item())

# Manual check with log_softmax
log_probs = torch.log_softmax(logits, dim=-1)
print("manual:", (-log_probs[0, target]).item())
snippets.explanation: CrossEntropyLoss applies log-softmax internally and averages −log q(y) over the batch.

snippets.title: Binary cross-entropy (Bernoulli)
snippets.code:
import numpy as np

y = 1          # true label
p_hat = 0.2    # predicted P(y=1)

bce = -(y * np.log(p_hat) + (1 - y) * np.log(1 - p_hat))
print("BCE:", bce)

# sklearn equivalent
from sklearn.metrics import log_loss
print("log_loss:", log_loss([y], [[1-p_hat, p_hat]]))
snippets.explanation: Binary cross-entropy is H(P, Q) for Bernoulli P (label) and Bernoulli Q (sigmoid output).
